title: Firmware Request: Increased BLE Transmit Strength for Repeaters when using BLE during OTA

messages.author: Submarine_Man
messages.text: The nRF52840 supports a transmit power level of up to 8 maximum. The default value for BLE_TX_POWER is 4 in the SerialBLEInterface.h file. BLE is normally disabled in a repeater, except when it is placed in OTA mode. In OTA mode, BLE is enabled to allow for the transfer of the file during the "Over the Air" firmware update. Updating a repeater using BLE OTA eliminates the need to physically connect to the repeater (bring it down, reflash and return it, which can be "risky" on a highly slanted roof). The increased power can increase the range of the BLE connection (it may be high up on your roof). Can the default value be changed to 8 to increase the BLE signal strength for repeaters that utilize the nRF52840 during an OTA firmware update? The power utilization impact of the increased transmit signal is minimal since this mode is only used during a Firmware update. A low BLE signal level can slow the speed of the data transfer, an increase in transmit signal can improve speed and the success rate of an OTA update. Once placed in OTA mode the repeater must successfully complete the transfer, with a weak BLE link the connection can be dropped, once dropped the repeater will remain in the "DFU/Bootloader" state and must be Flashed via the USB-C interface to load the firmware and recover from "getting stuck" in OTA mode.
messages.content_html: The nRF52840 supports a transmit power level of up to 8 maximum. The default value for BLE_TX_POWER is 4 in the SerialBLEInterface.h file. BLE is normally disabled in a repeater, except when it is placed in OTA mode. In OTA mode, BLE is enabled to allow for the transfer of the file during the "Over the Air" firmware update. Updating a repeater using BLE OTA eliminates the need to physically connect to the repeater (bring it down, reflash and return it, which can be "risky" on a highly slanted roof). The increased power can increase the range of the BLE connection (it may be high up on your roof). Can the default value be changed to 8 to increase the BLE signal strength for repeaters that utilize the nRF52840 during an OTA firmware update? The power utilization impact of the increased transmit signal is minimal since this mode is only used during a Firmware update. A low BLE signal level can slow the speed of the data transfer, an increase in transmit signal can improve speed and the success rate of an OTA update. Once placed in OTA mode the repeater must successfully complete the transfer, with a weak BLE link the connection can be dropped, once dropped the repeater will remain in the "DFU/Bootloader" state and must be Flashed via the USB-C interface to load the firmware and recover from "getting stuck" in OTA mode.
messages.reactions_html: 4
